danger_signals.visual_form: Unit sprites/models rendered in enemy faction colors and visual styles
danger_signals.meaning: Active hostile threat requiring tactical response

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual feedback on units being suppressed (reduced accuracy animation/effect)
danger_signals.meaning: Unit is under fire and combat effectiveness is degraded

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy units positioned on elevated terrain with clear line-of-sight advantage
danger_signals.meaning: Tactical vulnerability; enemy has accuracy/range advantage and can target weak points (topside) of armored units

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual degradation of unit models (damage indicators, visual wear)
danger_signals.meaning: Unit integrity compromised; continued exposure increases elimination risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy units concealed in shadows or foliage before engagement
danger_signals.meaning: Undetected threat; scouting failure creates vulnerability to coordinated attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple units of same faction positioned in formation
safety_signals.meaning: Mutual support available; reduced isolation vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Player units positioned on elevated terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Increased accuracy, sight range, and tactical vantage for topside armor strikes

safety_signals.visual_form: Anti-air, repair, or medical units nearby primary combat units
safety_signals.meaning: Specialized support available; role-specific vulnerabilities mitigated

safety_signals.visual_form: Infantry units positioned in shadows or foliage
safety_signals.meaning: Unit camouflaged from enemy detection; reduced engagement risk before first strike

safety_signals.visual_form: Units positioned to side/rear of enemy armored units rather than frontal
safety_signals.meaning: Attack against weak points; armor less effective from these angles

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Heavy armor aesthetic; traditional ballistics weaponry; ground-based movement; hardier unit construction; Jaeger Infantry (four-man sniper squads)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Unit visual language emphasizes durability and conventional military doctrine

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hover propulsion system visual (floating stance); energy weaponry (distinct visual effects); faster/more mobile unit silhouettes; Templar Infantry (all-female anti-tank specialists with missile launchers)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Unit visual language emphasizes speed, energy technology, and specialized anti-armor capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Orbital dropships visible delivering units to battlefield; units emerging from dropship landing zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units begin in staged deployment positions; no reinforcement mechanic available after initial drop

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: No resource gathering or base construction visible; only pre-loaded squad composition manifest
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players must manage finite squad loadout; no economic expansion possible; limited special weapon uses (typically 2-3 per mission, single-use for high-impact abilities)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High ground areas clearly distinguishable; shadow/foliage areas visually distinct for concealment potential
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unit accuracy, sight range, and armor vulnerability change based on elevation and positioning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct visual models for vehicle, support, aircraft, and infantry categories; unique silhouettes per unit type; faction-specific unit variants visually distinct
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each unit type exhibits unique strengths/weaknesses; players must coordinate multi-role squads to achieve objectives

environmental_storytelling.example: Crayven units display heavy armor, traditional ballistics, ground-based movement; Order of the New Dawn units display hover systems, energy weapons, increased speed — visual design communicates philosophical and technological differences without exposition

High ground positions, shadows, foliage, and armor weak points (topside) are all visually presented as environmental storytelling elements that communicate optimal tactical positioning and vulnerability states
Squad loadout screen and pre-mission configuration show how player choices materialize on battlefield; special weapons and equipment choices are visually distinct on units
Bird's-eye to ground-level camera perspective allows players to perceive battlefield topology, concealment opportunities, and line-of-sight factors — visual communication of tactical possibilities

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: High armor effectiveness; resistant to damage from forward direction
Significantly reduced armor effectiveness; vulnerable to flanking and high-ground attacks
Reduced accuracy in return fire; visual degradation in attack effectiveness
Camouflaged; undetected by enemy until direct engagement or scout reconnaissance
Four-man sniper squad; effective for reconnaissance and target identification

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Order of the New Dawn Templar Infantry
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: All-female squadron with powerful anti-tank missile launchers; cannot attack infantry targets; specialized anti-armor combat role
Can restore unit integrity through repair kits or medkits; finite uses per mission
Provide protective fire against aerial units; role-specific counter to aircraft threats

notes: Ground Control communicates world-state primarily through asymmetric faction visual design and tactical terrain features rather than dynamic environmental changes. The absence of resource management and reinforcement mechanics is itself a visual/systemic communication: no base structures, no resource flows, no economic visualization. Faction identity is conveyed through unit aesthetic (armor vs. hover, ballistics vs. energy), specialist unit differences (snipers vs. anti-tank), and implied technological/philosophical differences. Danger is communicated through spatial positioning (high ground, concealment), armor weakness states, and suppression effects. The game emphasizes player perception of tactical opportunity rather than narrative environmental storytelling.
